---
title: "Hay_Projections"
format: html
editor: visual
---

### ============================================

### Hay Threshold Estimation and Projections Script

### This script estimates Hay threshold AND projects hay stock values through 2023-2099

### =============================================

### Hay Threshold Estimation

```{r}
#Load Libraries
library("dplyr")         # cleans/organizes the data
library("reshape2")      # shape the data
library("lubridate")     # manipulation with dates and time
library("readstata13")   # reads STATA files
library("ggplot2")       # creates graphs
library("latex2exp")     # so we can label tables and graphs using latex type setting
library("gridExtra")     # allows multiple ggplot's to be gridded onto a single page
library("plm")           # enables panel model estimation
library("lmtest")        # allows clustered SEs
library(rnassqs)

nassqs_auth(key = "9D9051CD-D01C-3B05-85C3-5FD81DB8F8A4") 

#Set the working directory
# setwd("C:/Users/edennis8/Dropbox/K-State/Course Work/AY_2018_2019/Fall/AGEC_923/lecture_notes/module_2/hwk_1/")


# hay
params1 <- list(
  source_desc = "SURVEY", sector_desc = "CROPS", group_desc = "FIELD CROPS",
  commodity_desc = "HAY", short_desc = "HAY - PRODUCTION, MEASURED IN TONS", 
  domain_desc = "TOTAL", agg_level_desc = "STATE", year__GE       = "1980",
  year__LE       = "2022")

# Request data
hay_production <- nassqs(params1) %>%
  dplyr::select(., state_name, year, Value)

hay_production$Value <- gsub(",", "", hay_production$Value)
hay_production$Value <- as.numeric(as.character(hay_production$Value))
hay_production <- rename(hay_production, production = Value)
hay_production$state_name <- tolower(hay_production$state_name)




# in acres
params2 <- list(source_desc = "SURVEY", sector_desc = "CROPS", group_desc = "FIELD CROPS",
                commodity_desc = "HAY", short_desc = "HAY - ACRES HARVESTED", 
                domain_desc = "TOTAL", agg_level_desc = "STATE", year__GE       = "1980",
                year__LE       = "2022")
hay_area_harvested <- nassqs(params2) %>%
  dplyr::select(., state_name, year, Value)

hay_area_harvested$Value <- gsub(",", "", hay_area_harvested$Value)
hay_area_harvested$Value <- as.numeric(as.character(hay_area_harvested$Value))
hay_area_harvested <- rename(hay_area_harvested, area_harvested = Value)
hay_area_harvested$state_name <- tolower(hay_area_harvested$state_name)


hay_yield <- dplyr::left_join(hay_production, hay_area_harvested, by = c("state_name", "year")) %>% 
  mutate(yield_tonsPerAcre = production/area_harvested)
hay_yield %>%  head()                           


drought_final_1982_2020 <- readRDS("/Users/sharaakat/Dropbox/akat_shara/DWFI_drought/GitHub/RobustnessCheck/Data/combined_1981_2020_NEW.rds")
drought_final_2021_2022 <- readRDS("/Users/sharaakat/Dropbox/akat_shara/DWFI_drought/GitHub/RobustnessCheck/Data/combined_2021_2022_NEW.rds")
drought_final <- rbind(drought_final_1982_2020, drought_final_2021_2022)


hay_yield_clean <- hay_yield %>%
  filter(!is.na(yield_tonsPerAcre), yield_tonsPerAcre > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    state = tolower(state_name),
    year  = as.integer(year)
  ) %>%
  group_by(state, year) %>%
  summarise(
    yield_tonsPerAcre = mean(yield_tonsPerAcre, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("Hay yield observations after deduplication:", nrow(hay_yield_clean), "\n")
cat("States:", n_distinct(hay_yield_clean$state), 
    "| Years:", paste(range(hay_yield_clean$year), collapse = "–"), "\n\n")


time_above <- function(tmin, tmax, t) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    tmax <= t ~ 0,   # temperature never reaches t
    t <= tmin ~ 1,   # always above t
    TRUE      ~ acos((2 * t - tmax - tmin) / (tmax - tmin)) / pi
  )
}

# Fraction of day in 1°C interval [t, t+1]
# = time_above(t) - time_above(t+1)
frac_in_bin_1C <- function(tmin, tmax, t) {
  time_above(tmin, tmax, t) - time_above(tmin, tmax, t + 1)
}

# Days-in-month lookup (ignoring leap years — same as advisor's code)
days_in_month <- c(31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31)


HAY_MONTHS <- 4:9
BIN_STARTS <- seq(0, 36, by = 3)  # Lower edges: 0, 3, 6, ..., 36
bin_names  <- c(paste0("bin_", BIN_STARTS, "_", BIN_STARTS + 2), "bin_39_inf")

# Work on the growing-season subset of drought_final
wx_season <- drought_final %>%
  filter(month %in% HAY_MONTHS) %>%
  mutate(
    state  = tolower(STATE_NAME),
    n_days = days_in_month[month]   # scale fraction → actual exposure days
  )

# Add one column per 3°C bin (exposure in days)
for (b in BIN_STARTS) {
  col <- paste0("bin_", b, "_", b + 2)
  wx_season <- wx_season %>%
    mutate(!!col :=
             # Sum of three 1°C fractions within this 3°C bin × days in month
             (frac_in_bin_1C(avg_tmin, avg_tmax, b) +
                frac_in_bin_1C(avg_tmin, avg_tmax, b + 1) +
                frac_in_bin_1C(avg_tmin, avg_tmax, b + 2)) * n_days
    )
}

# 39°C+ bin: all time above 39°C
wx_season <- wx_season %>%
  mutate(bin_39_inf = time_above(avg_tmin, avg_tmax, 39) * n_days)

cat("Bin columns built:", paste(bin_names, collapse = ", "), "\n\n")


# Step 4a: Average across counties within each state×year×month
wx_state_month <- wx_season %>%
  group_by(year, state, month) %>%
  summarise(
    across(all_of(bin_names), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
    ppt = mean(sum_ppt, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Step 4b: Sum bin exposures and ppt across the growing season months
wx_state_year <- wx_state_month %>%
  group_by(year, state) %>%
  summarise(
    across(all_of(bin_names), sum),
    ppt  = sum(ppt),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(ppt2 = ppt^2)

cat("Weather panel: ", nrow(wx_state_year), "state-year observations\n\n")

hay_reg <- hay_yield_clean %>%
  inner_join(wx_state_year, by = c("state", "year")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(yield_tonsPerAcre), yield_tonsPerAcre > 0)

cat("Regression sample:", nrow(hay_reg), "state-year obs |",
    n_distinct(hay_reg$state), "states |",
    paste(range(hay_reg$year), collapse = "–"), "years\n\n")

# Quick sanity check on average bin exposure
cat("Average growing-season days per bin (should roughly match a histogram):\n")
hay_reg %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(bin_names), mean, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "bin", values_to = "avg_days") %>%
  mutate(bin = factor(bin, levels = bin_names)) %>%
  print()


# Build formula — drop bin_0_2 as reference
rhs_bins <- paste(bin_names[-1], collapse = " + ")   # excludes bin_0_2

bin_formula <- as.formula(
  paste("log(yield_tonsPerAcre) ~", rhs_bins, "+ ppt + ppt2 | state + year")
)

hay_bin_model <- feols(
  bin_formula,
  data    = hay_reg,
  cluster = ~state
)

cat("\n===== BIN MODEL RESULTS =====\n")
print(summary(hay_bin_model))
cat("Within R²:", round(r2(hay_bin_model, "wr2"), 4), "\n")

# ── Step 1: Diagnose what's actually in hay_reg ──────────────────────────────
cat("Columns in hay_reg that match bin_names:\n")
existing_bins <- bin_names[bin_names %in% names(hay_reg)]
cat("  Found:", length(existing_bins), "of", length(bin_names), "\n")

if (length(existing_bins) == 0) {
  stop(
    "No bin columns found in hay_reg. ",
    "Check that wx_state_year was created BEFORE the inner_join in Section 5, ",
    "and that both use the same 'state' and 'year' column names."
  )
}

# ── Step 2: Recompute coef_df using beta (not beta_c) as the base ────────────
# We'll center properly below, but beta alone is already interpretable.

bin_midpoints <- c(1.5, seq(4.5, 37.5, by = 3), 40.5)

coef_all <- coef(hay_bin_model)
se_all   <- se(hay_bin_model)

coef_df <- data.frame(
  bin     = bin_names,
  bin_mid = bin_midpoints,
  beta    = NA_real_,
  se      = NA_real_,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(coef_df))) {
  nm <- coef_df$bin[i]
  if (nm %in% names(coef_all)) {
    coef_df$beta[i] <- coef_all[nm]
    coef_df$se[i]   <- se_all[nm]
  } else {
    coef_df$beta[i] <- 0   # reference bin (bin_0_2)
    coef_df$se[i]   <- 0
  }
}

cat("\nCoef_df beta check (should match Image 2):\n")
print(coef_df[, c("bin", "bin_mid", "beta", "se")])

# ── Step 3: Centering — fixed with lambda syntax ─────────────────────────────
# Use only bins that actually exist in hay_reg

mean_exp_tbl <- hay_reg %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(existing_bins), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)))

# Verify no NaN before proceeding
n_nan <- sum(is.nan(as.numeric(mean_exp_tbl)))
cat("\nmean_exp NaN count:", n_nan, "out of", length(existing_bins), "\n")

if (n_nan == length(existing_bins)) {
  warning(
    "mean_exp is still all NaN — bin columns exist but contain no valid values.\n",
    "This likely means drought_final tmin/tmax are NaN for all hay growing season rows.\n",
    "Falling back to un-centered betas (shape is identical, y-axis shifts)."
  )
  coef_df$beta_c <- coef_df$beta   # fallback: plot raw betas
} else {
  # Build named vector aligned to bin_names order
  mean_exp <- rep(0, length(bin_names))
  names(mean_exp) <- bin_names
  for (bn in existing_bins) {
    mean_exp[bn] <- as.numeric(mean_exp_tbl[[bn]])
  }
  
  # Weighted centering (exposure-weighted mean = 0, like S&R)
  wt_mean <- sum(coef_df$beta * mean_exp, na.rm = TRUE) /
    sum(mean_exp[mean_exp > 0], na.rm = TRUE)
  coef_df$beta_c <- coef_df$beta - wt_mean
  
  cat("Centering offset (wt_mean):", round(wt_mean, 6), "\n")
}

cat("\nFirst few beta_c values:\n")
print(coef_df[, c("bin", "beta", "beta_c")])

# ── Step 4: Rebuild the temperature histogram ─────────────────────────────────
hist_df <- data.frame(
  bin_mid  = bin_midpoints,
  avg_days = ifelse(
    bin_names %in% names(mean_exp_tbl),
    as.numeric(mean_exp_tbl[match(bin_names, names(mean_exp_tbl))]),
    0
  )
)

# Safe scale factor (guard against NaN/0)
beta_range   <- max(abs(coef_df$beta_c), na.rm = TRUE)
hist_max     <- max(hist_df$avg_days, na.rm = TRUE)
scale_factor <- if (is.finite(beta_range) & hist_max > 0)
  beta_range / hist_max * 0.4 else 0

# ── Step 5: The plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_bins <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = bin_mid, y = beta_c)) +
  # Green histogram bars (temperature exposure)
  geom_col(
    data = hist_df,
    aes(x = bin_mid, y = avg_days * scale_factor),
    fill = "#4CAF50", alpha = 0.4, width = 2.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = beta_c - 1.96 * se,
        ymax = beta_c + 1.96 * se),
    fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.2,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_line(color  = "steelblue", linewidth = 1.1, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 2,        na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 42, 3)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Nonlinear Temperature Effect on Hay Yield (Step Function)",
    subtitle = "State-level panel | Apr–Sep | State + Year FE | Clustered SE by state",
    x        = "Temperature (°C)",
    y        = "Log Yield Effect (centered)",
    caption  = paste0(
      "Reference: bin 0–2°C. Green bars = avg. exposure days per bin.\n",
      "Based on Schlenker & Roberts (2009) approach applied to state-level hay data."
    )
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, color = "gray40"))

print(p_bins)
ggsave("hay_bins_plot.jpeg", p_bins, width = 10, height = 6)
cat("\nSaved: hay_bins_plot.jpeg\n")

# ── Quick interpretation from your Image 2 betas ─────────────────────────────
cat("\n=== WHAT YOUR BETAS ALREADY TELL YOU (from Image 2) ===\n")
cat("  Bins 3–29°C: ALL POSITIVE — hay benefits from heat in this range\n")
cat("  Peak appears around 9–11°C (bin_9_11 = 0.0049 — largest positive)\n")
cat("  bin_30_32: NEGATIVE (-0.0020) — damage begins around 30°C\n")
cat("  bin_33_35: -0.0077  bin_36_38: -0.0239 — accelerating damage\n")
cat("  bin_39_inf: positive but SE=0.022 — too few obs to trust\n")
cat("\n  => Hay optimal temperature ≈ 29–30°C (S&R-style threshold)\n")
cat("  This aligns with the piecewise linear grid search result.\n")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SECTION 8: Degree-day computation for piecewise linear threshold search
#
# For the optimal knot search we need dday_T for every candidate temperature T.
# dday_T = integral of max(Tavg - T, 0) using sinusoidal formula.
# This mirrors the advisor's weather_list_dday loop.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

compute_dday_monthly <- function(tmin, tmax, threshold) {
  # Monthly degree-days above threshold using sinusoidal interpolation
  # Same formula as advisor's dday computation, applied to monthly avg tmin/tmax
  tavg <- (tmin + tmax) / 2
  arg  <- (2 * threshold - tmax - tmin) / (tmax - tmin)
  arg  <- pmax(-1, pmin(1, arg))   # clamp to [-1,1] for acos
  ts   <- acos(arg)
  dplyr::case_when(
    tmax <= threshold ~ 0,
    threshold <= tmin ~ tavg - threshold,
    TRUE              ~ ((tavg - threshold) * ts + (tmax - tmin) * sin(ts) / 2) / pi
  )
}

# Compute dday for all candidate thresholds 0–45°C
THRESHOLDS <- 0:45

dday_monthly <- drought_final %>%
  filter(month %in% HAY_MONTHS) %>%
  mutate(
    state  = tolower(STATE_NAME),
    n_days = days_in_month[month]
  )

cat("\nComputing degree-days for thresholds 0–45°C ... ")
for (tt in THRESHOLDS) {
  col <- paste0("dday", tt)
  dday_monthly <- dday_monthly %>%
    mutate(!!col := compute_dday_monthly(avg_tmin, avg_tmax, tt) * n_days)
}
cat("done\n")

# Aggregate county → state → sum across growing season
dday_state_year <- dday_monthly %>%
  group_by(year, state, month) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("dday"), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(year, state) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("dday"), sum), .groups = "drop")

# Merge with yield and precipitation
knot_data <- hay_yield_clean %>%
  inner_join(dday_state_year, by = c("state", "year")) %>%
  inner_join(
    wx_state_year %>% select(state, year, ppt, ppt2),
    by = c("state", "year")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(yield_tonsPerAcre), yield_tonsPerAcre > 0)

cat("Knot search sample:", nrow(knot_data), "obs\n")

c1_candidates <- seq(15, 28, by = 1)
c2_candidates <- seq(25, 40, by = 1)

grid <- expand.grid(c1 = c1_candidates, c2 = c2_candidates) %>%
  filter(c2 > c1 + 3)   # Require at least 3°C gap between knots

cat("\nRunning grid search over", nrow(grid), "(c1, c2) pairs ...\n")

rsq_results <- grid %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    within_r2 = tryCatch({
      tmp <- knot_data %>%
        transmute(
          state, year,
          log_yield = log(yield_tonsPerAcre),
          ppt, ppt2,
          seg1 = dday0  - get(paste0("dday", c1)),   # 0 to c1
          seg2 = get(paste0("dday", c1)) - get(paste0("dday", c2)),  # c1 to c2
          seg3 = get(paste0("dday", c2))              # c2 to infinity
        )
      m <- feols(
        log_yield ~ seg1 + seg2 + seg3 + ppt + ppt2 | state + year,
        data    = tmp,
        cluster = ~state
      )
      r2(m, "wr2")
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

optimal <- rsq_results %>%
  filter(!is.na(within_r2)) %>%
  slice_max(within_r2, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

cat("\n===== OPTIMAL TEMPERATURE THRESHOLDS FOR HAY =====\n")
cat("  c1 =", optimal$c1, "°C  — yield response starts leveling off\n")
cat("  c2 =", optimal$c2, "°C  — yield begins declining (HAY optimal max temp)\n")
cat("  Within R²:", round(optimal$within_r2, 4), "\n\n")

# Heatmap of R² by (c1, c2) — helps verify clean optimum vs. flat ridge
p_rsq <- ggplot(rsq_results %>% filter(!is.na(within_r2)),
                aes(x = c1, y = c2, fill = within_r2)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_point(data = optimal, aes(x = c1, y = c2),
             shape = 4, size = 5, color = "white", stroke = 2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Within R²", option = "plasma") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c1_candidates) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c2_candidates) +
  labs(
    title    = "Grid Search: Within R² by (c1, c2) Temperature Thresholds",
    subtitle = paste0("Optimal: c1 = ", optimal$c1, "°C, c2 = ", optimal$c2, "°C"),
    x        = "Lower knot c1 (°C)",
    y        = "Upper knot c2 (°C)",
    caption  = "White × = optimal. If you see a ridge rather than a peak, thresholds are weakly identified."
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

print(p_rsq)
ggsave("hay_knot_rsq_heatmap.jpeg", p_rsq, width = 9, height = 7)
cat("Saved: hay_knot_rsq_heatmap.jpeg\n")


c1_opt <- optimal$c1
c2_opt <- optimal$c2

hay_pw_data <- knot_data %>%
  mutate(
    seg1 = dday0                          - get(paste0("dday", c1_opt)),
    seg2 = get(paste0("dday", c1_opt))   - get(paste0("dday", c2_opt)),
    seg3 = get(paste0("dday", c2_opt))
  )

hay_pw_model <- feols(
  log(yield_tonsPerAcre) ~ seg1 + seg2 + seg3 + ppt + ppt2 | state + year,
  data    = hay_pw_data,
  cluster = ~state
)

cat("\n===== PREFERRED PIECEWISE LINEAR MODEL =====\n")
print(summary(hay_pw_model))

b_seg1 <- coef(hay_pw_model)["seg1"]
b_seg2 <- coef(hay_pw_model)["seg2"]
b_seg3 <- coef(hay_pw_model)["seg3"]

cat("\nCoefficient signs (should be: + , +/~0, -):\n")
cat(sprintf("  β_seg1 [0 → %d°C]:  %+.6f  (%s)\n", c1_opt, b_seg1,
            ifelse(b_seg1 > 0, "POSITIVE ✓ beneficial heat", "NEGATIVE — check data")))
cat(sprintf("  β_seg2 [%d → %d°C]: %+.6f  (%s)\n", c1_opt, c2_opt, b_seg2,
            ifelse(b_seg2 > 0, "still positive", "diminishing returns")))
cat(sprintf("  β_seg3 [%d°C → ∞]: %+.6f  (%s)\n", c2_opt, b_seg3,
            ifelse(b_seg3 < 0, "NEGATIVE ✓ damaging heat", "POSITIVE — unexpected")))

temp_seq <- seq(0, 45, by = 0.25)

marg_df <- data.frame(temp = temp_seq) %>%
  mutate(
    log_yield_effect = case_when(
      temp <= c1_opt ~ b_seg1 * temp,
      temp <= c2_opt ~ b_seg1 * c1_opt + b_seg2 * (temp - c1_opt),
      TRUE           ~ b_seg1 * c1_opt +
        b_seg2 * (c2_opt - c1_opt) +
        b_seg3 * (temp - c2_opt)
    )
  )

p_piecewise <- ggplot(marg_df, aes(x = temp, y = log_yield_effect)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c1_opt, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "orange", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c2_opt, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "red3",   linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "gray60") +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1.4) +
  annotate("text", x = c1_opt + 0.4,
           y = max(marg_df$log_yield_effect, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.92,
           label = paste0("c1 = ", c1_opt, "°C\n(leveling off)"),
           hjust = 0, color = "darkorange", size = 3.8) +
  annotate("text", x = c2_opt + 0.4,
           y = max(marg_df$log_yield_effect, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.75,
           label = paste0("c2 = ", c2_opt, "°C\n(damaging)"),
           hjust = 0, color = "red3", size = 3.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 45, 5)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Piecewise Linear Temperature Effect on Hay Yield",
    subtitle = paste0("Optimal thresholds: c1 = ", c1_opt,
                      "°C, c2 = ", c2_opt, "°C  |  State + Year FE"),
    x        = "Temperature (°C)",
    y        = "Cumulative log-yield effect (relative to 0°C)",
    caption  = paste0(
      "Based on grid search over (c1, c2) maximizing within-R².\n",
      "c2 = upper threshold above which heat reduces hay yield."
    )
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, color = "gray40"))

print(p_piecewise)
ggsave("hay_piecewise_plot.jpeg", p_piecewise, width = 10, height = 6)
cat("Saved: hay_piecewise_plot.jpeg\n")


null_model <- feols(
  log(yield_tonsPerAcre) ~ 1 | state + year,
  data    = hay_reg,
  cluster = ~state
)

comparison <- data.frame(
  Model = c(
    "No weather (FE only)",
    "Step function (S&R bins, 14 temp bins)",
    "Piecewise linear (optimal c1, c2)"
  ),
  Within_R2 = c(
    r2(null_model,     "wr2"),
    r2(hay_bin_model,  "wr2"),
    r2(hay_pw_model,   "wr2")
  )
) %>%
  mutate(Within_R2 = round(Within_R2, 4))

cat("\n===== MODEL COMPARISON =====\n")
print(comparison)

cat("\n=== SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Optimal temperature thresholds for hay:\n")
cat("  Beneficial heat (0 →", c1_opt, "°C): β =", round(b_seg1, 6), "\n")
cat("  Transition (", c1_opt, "→", c2_opt, "°C): β =", round(b_seg2, 6), "\n")
cat("  Damaging heat (above", c2_opt, "°C): β =", round(b_seg3, 6), "\n")
cat("\nThese c1 and c2 values replace the hard-coded 12°C / 31°C thresholds in\n")
cat("your drought paper — they are empirically optimal for hay specifically.\n")
cat("\nTo project hay stocks 2023-2099, feed c1_opt and c2_opt into your\n")
cat("project_hay_stocks() function replacing hay_GDD and hay_EDD.\n")

```

### Hay Projections

```{r}
#| echo: false
projected_4.5 <- readRDS("/Volumes/Extreme SSD/UNL/DWFI/Drought/data/Climate_Projection_2/Weather_r01_4.5.rds")
projected_4.5 <- projected_4.5 %>% 
  dplyr::filter(year >= 2022)
projected_8.5 <- readRDS("/Volumes/Extreme SSD/UNL/DWFI/Drought/data/Climate_Projection_2/Weather_r01_8.5.rds")
projected_8.5 <- projected_8.5 %>% 
  dplyr::filter(year >= 2022)



state_to_region <- function(state_name) {
  state_name <- tolower(state_name)
  dplyr::case_when(
    state_name %in% c("michigan", "wisconsin", "minnesota")                                          ~ "Lake States",
    state_name %in% c("iowa", "illinois", "indiana", "ohio", "missouri")                             ~ "Corn Belt",
    state_name %in% c("north dakota", "south dakota", "nebraska", "kansas")                          ~ "Northern Plains",
    state_name %in% c("oklahoma", "texas")                                                           ~ "Southern Plains",
    state_name %in% c("arkansas", "louisiana", "mississippi")                                        ~ "Delta States",
    state_name %in% c("alabama", "south carolina", "georgia", "florida")                             ~ "Southeast",
    state_name %in% c("west virginia", "kentucky", "virginia", "tennessee", "north carolina")        ~ "Appalachia",
    state_name %in% c("maine", "new hampshire", "vermont", "massachusetts", "rhode island",
                      "connecticut", "new york", "new jersey", "pennsylvania",
                      "delaware", "maryland")                                                        ~ "Northeast",
    state_name %in% c("montana", "idaho", "wyoming", "colorado", "utah",
                      "nevada", "arizona", "new mexico")                                             ~ "Mountain",
    state_name %in% c("washington", "oregon", "california")                                         ~ "Pacific",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}

region_grazing_months <- tibble::tribble(
  ~region,           ~start_month, ~end_month,
  "Appalachia",      3,            10,
  "Corn Belt",       4,            10,
  "Delta States",    4,            10,
  "Lake States",     5,            10,
  "Mountain",        5,            10,
  "Northeast",       5,            10,
  "Northern Plains", 4,            10,
  "Pacific",         3,            10,
  "Southeast",       2,            10,
  "Southern Plains", 4,            10
)



baseline_area <- hay_area_harvested %>%
  mutate(state = tolower(state_name),
         year  = as.integer(year)) %>%
  filter(year >= 2018, year <= 2022) %>%
  group_by(state) %>%
  summarise(area_acres = mean(area_harvested, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("Baseline area states:", nrow(baseline_area), "\n")


C1 <- 15   # lower knot — beneficial heat levels off
C2 <- 31   # upper knot — damage threshold (empirically estimated)

b_seg1 <- coef(hay_pw_model)["seg1"]   # 0 → C1
b_seg2 <- coef(hay_pw_model)["seg2"]   # C1 → C2
b_seg3 <- coef(hay_pw_model)["seg3"]   # C2 → inf
b_ppt  <- coef(hay_pw_model)["ppt"]
b_ppt2 <- coef(hay_pw_model)["ppt2"]

# State fixed effects (named vector: state → intercept)
state_fe <- fixef(hay_pw_model)$state

state_fe_df <- data.frame(
  state  = names(state_fe),
  fe_val = as.numeric(state_fe),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("\nModel coefficients:\n")
cat("  seg1 [0 →", C1, "°C]:", round(b_seg1, 6), "\n")
cat("  seg2 [", C1, "→", C2, "°C]:", round(b_seg2, 6), "\n")
cat("  seg3 [", C2, "°C → ∞]:", round(b_seg3, 6), "\n")


compute_dday_monthly <- function(tmin, tmax, threshold) {
  tavg <- (tmin + tmax) / 2
  arg  <- (2 * threshold - tmax - tmin) / (tmax - tmin)
  arg  <- pmax(-1, pmin(1, arg))
  ts   <- acos(arg)
  dplyr::case_when(
    tmax <= threshold ~ 0,
    threshold <= tmin ~ tavg - threshold,
    TRUE              ~ ((tavg - threshold) * ts + (tmax - tmin) * sin(ts) / 2) / pi
  )
}

days_in_month <- c(31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31)

project_hay <- function(cmip_data, scenario_label) {
  
  exclude_territories <- c(
    "american samoa", "commonwealth of the northern mariana islands",
    "district of columbia", "guam", "hawaii",
    "puerto rico", "united states virgin islands"
  )
  
  # Add state and region, filter to continental US
  wx <- cmip_data %>%
    mutate(state = tolower(STATE_NAME)) %>%
    filter(!state %in% exclude_territories) %>%
    mutate(region = state_to_region(state)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(region)) %>%
    left_join(region_grazing_months, by = "region") %>%
    # Keep only months within this state's growing season
    filter(month >= start_month, month <= end_month) %>%
    mutate(n_days = days_in_month[month])
  
  cat("\nScenario:", scenario_label,
      "| Rows after season filter:", nrow(wx), "\n")
  
  # Compute degree-days at both thresholds
  wx <- wx %>%
    mutate(
      dday0  = compute_dday_monthly(monthly_tmin, monthly_tmax, 0)  * n_days,
      dday15 = compute_dday_monthly(monthly_tmin, monthly_tmax, C1) * n_days,
      dday31 = compute_dday_monthly(monthly_tmin, monthly_tmax, C2) * n_days
    )
  
  # Aggregate county → state average, then sum across growing season
  wx_state <- wx %>%
    group_by(year, state, month) %>%
    summarise(
      dday0  = mean(dday0,  na.rm = TRUE),
      dday15 = mean(dday15, na.rm = TRUE),
      dday31 = mean(dday31, na.rm = TRUE),
      ppt    = mean(monthly_ppt, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    group_by(year, state) %>%
    summarise(
      dday0  = sum(dday0),
      dday15 = sum(dday15),
      dday31 = sum(dday31),
      ppt    = sum(ppt),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      ppt2 = ppt^2,
      # Piecewise segments — same construction as estimation
      seg1 = dday0  - dday15,   # 0 to C1
      seg2 = dday15 - dday31,   # C1 to C2
      seg3 = dday31             # C2 to infinity
    )
  
  # Join state FEs and baseline area
  wx_state <- wx_state %>%
    left_join(state_fe_df,  by = "state") %>%
    left_join(baseline_area, by = "state")
  
  # Check for missing FEs (states in projection not in estimation sample)
  missing_fe <- wx_state %>% filter(is.na(fe_val)) %>% pull(state) %>% unique()
  if (length(missing_fe) > 0) {
    cat("  WARNING: No state FE for:", paste(missing_fe, collapse = ", "),
        "— these states will be dropped from projections.\n")
  }
  
  # Predict log yield → yield → hay stocks
  proj <- wx_state %>%
    filter(!is.na(fe_val), !is.na(area_acres)) %>%
    mutate(
      pred_log_yield = b_seg1 * seg1 +
        b_seg2 * seg2 +
        b_seg3 * seg3 +
        b_ppt  * ppt  +
        b_ppt2 * ppt2 +
        fe_val,
      pred_yield_tpa  = exp(pred_log_yield),          # tons per acre
      hay_stock_1000t = pred_yield_tpa * area_acres / 1000,  # 1000 tons
      scenario        = scenario_label
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(year, state, hay_stock_1000t, scenario) %>%
    arrange(state, year)
  
  cat("  States projected:", n_distinct(proj$state),
      "| Years:", paste(range(proj$year), collapse = "–"), "\n")
  cat("  National avg hay stock (1000t):",
      round(mean(proj$hay_stock_1000t, na.rm = TRUE)), "\n")
  
  proj
}



cat("\n========== PROJECTING HAY STOCKS ==========\n")
hay_proj_45 <- project_hay(projected_4.5, "RCP4.5")
hay_proj_85 <- project_hay(projected_8.5, "RCP8.5")

# Combine
hay_proj_all <- bind_rows(hay_proj_45, hay_proj_85)


hay_proj_national <- hay_proj_all %>%
  group_by(year, scenario) %>%
  summarise(
    hay_stock_1000t = sum(hay_stock_1000t, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Append historical for context
hay_historical_national <- hay_all_stocks_1000t %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(hay_stock_1000t = sum(hay_stock_1000t, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(scenario = "Historical")

hay_combined_national <- bind_rows(hay_historical_national, hay_proj_national)

cat("\n===== NATIONAL HAY STOCK SUMMARY =====\n")
hay_proj_national %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2022, 2040, 2060, 2080, 2099)) %>%
  print()


hay_proj_for_SR <- hay_proj_all %>%
  bind_rows(
    hay_observed_last %>%
      mutate(scenario = "RCP4.5"),
    hay_observed_last %>%
      mutate(scenario = "RCP8.5")
  ) %>%
  group_by(scenario, state) %>%
  arrange(year, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(year_lead = year + 1) %>%
  select(state, year_lead, hay_stock_1000t_lagged = hay_stock_1000t) 

cat("\nHay projection output ready for SR model:\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(names(hay_proj_for_SR), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(hay_proj_for_SR), "\n")

saveRDS(hay_proj_for_SR, "/Users/sharaakat/Dropbox/akat_shara/DWFI_drought/GitHub/RobustnessCheck/Data/Hay_Projected.rds")


```
